source.source_note: Visual page preview confirms headword and source direction: not based on clear logical thought; 不理性的/不合理的.

headline.core_en: not based on reason, logic, or evidence

familiar_bridge.summary: rational 是“有理由、讲逻辑”；irrational 就是想法或反应失去这个依据。

core.main: irrational 评价想法、恐惧、决定或行为缺少理性、逻辑或证据支撑；它强调判断过程站不住脚。

senses.label: 不理性的；不合逻辑的
senses.text: not based on reason, logic, or evidence

examples.sentence: His fear of flying is irrational because he knows the actual risk is very low.
examples.translation: 他害怕坐飞机是不理性的，因为他知道实际风险很低。
examples.note: 用 fear 体现“缺少理性依据”。

examples.sentence: The committee rejected the policy as irrational because it ignored all available evidence.
examples.translation: 委员会认为这项政策忽视了所有现有证据，因此不合逻辑。
examples.note: 政策和判断都可以被评价为 irrational。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: It is unreasonable to expect instant results.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: 更日常，强调要求、决定或行为不合情理。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: His argument sounds illogical.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: 强调推理结构不成立，语气比 irrational 更偏逻辑分析。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The fear was unfounded.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: 强调缺少事实或证据基础。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The conclusion rests on fallacious reasoning.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: 常用于论证或推理错误，比 irrational 更技术化。

antonyms.example_sentence: A rational decision is based on evidence.
antonyms.difference_note: 直接反义，强调基于理由和证据。

confusable_words.example_sentence: An emotional response is not always irrational.
confusable_words.difference_note: emotional 和 irrational 容易靠得很近；emotional 侧重“情绪化的”，irrational 侧重“不理性的”。

extension.root_affix: ir- 表示否定，rational 是“理性的”；irrational 就是“不理性的”。

word_family.example_sentence: The plan lacks rationality.
The policy has a clear rationale.

usage_groups.group_title: 不理性的恐惧、信念、决定或政策
usage_groups.meaning_focus: irrational 评价想法、恐惧、决定或行为缺少理性、逻辑或证据支撑；它强调判断过程站不住脚。

examples.sentence: His fear of flying is irrational because he knows the actual risk is very low.
examples.translation: 他害怕坐飞机是不理性的，因为他知道实际风险很低。
examples.teaching_note: 用 fear 体现“缺少理性依据”。

examples.sentence: The committee rejected the policy as irrational because it ignored all available evidence.
examples.translation: 委员会认为这项政策忽视了所有现有证据，因此不合逻辑。
examples.teaching_note: 政策和判断都可以被评价为 irrational。

source.source_note: Visual page preview confirms headword and source direction: present everywhere.

headline.core_en: present almost everywhere

familiar_bridge.summary: everywhere 是日常的“到处”；omnipresent 更像正式地说某种东西到处都在、躲不开。

core.main: omnipresent 强调某种影响、现象或力量几乎处处存在，让人很难避开。

senses.label: 无处不在的
senses.text: present almost everywhere

examples.sentence: Smartphones feel almost omnipresent on the subway.
examples.translation: 在地铁里，智能手机几乎让人感觉无处不在。
examples.note: 用学生熟悉的场景体会“到处都在、很难避开”。

examples.sentence: The essay criticizes the omnipresent influence of commercial media on public taste.
examples.translation: 这篇文章批评商业媒体对公众品味无处不在的影响。
examples.note: 学术语境常搭配 influence。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The problem is widespread.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: 强调分布广，不一定到处都在。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Smartphones are ubiquitous in cities.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: 最接近 omnipresent，常用于技术、现象或物品随处可见。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The influence of advertising is pervasive.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: 强调影响渗透到各处，常带有难以摆脱的感觉。

antonyms.example_sentence: Such events are rare.
antonyms.difference_note: 反向强调少见、罕见。

confusable_words.example_sentence: This word is common in daily English.
confusable_words.difference_note: common 和 omnipresent 容易靠得很近；common 侧重“常见的”，omnipresent 侧重“无处不在的”。

extension.root_affix: omni- 表示“全部、到处”；present 是“存在的、在场的”；合起来就是“到处都存在”。

word_family.example_sentence: The omnipresence of cameras changed public life.
Her presence made the room calmer.

related_words.example_sentence: The narrator seems omniscient.
Bears are omnivorous animals.

usage_groups.group_title: 无处不在的广告、影响或技术
usage_groups.meaning_focus: omnipresent 强调某种影响、现象或力量几乎处处存在，让人很难避开。

examples.sentence: Smartphones feel almost omnipresent on the subway.
examples.translation: 在地铁里，智能手机几乎让人感觉无处不在。
examples.teaching_note: 用学生熟悉的场景体会“到处都在、很难避开”。

examples.sentence: The essay criticizes the omnipresent influence of commercial media on public taste.
examples.translation: 这篇文章批评商业媒体对公众品味无处不在的影响。
examples.teaching_note: 学术语境常搭配 influence。

source.source_note: Visual page preview confirms headword and source direction: fixed patterns; too regular and unoriginal.

headline.core_en: following a fixed, predictable, and unoriginal pattern

familiar_bridge.summary: formula 不只指数学公式，也可以像“固定套路”；formulaic 就是太按套路来。

core.main: formulaic 批评表达、故事、写作或方法过于依赖固定模板，因此显得老套、缺少原创性。

senses.label: 公式化的；套板的
senses.text: following a fixed, predictable, and unoriginal pattern

examples.sentence: The speech sounded formulaic and failed to move the audience.
examples.translation: 这场演讲听起来很公式化，没能打动观众。
examples.note: 这里强调演讲像套模板，缺少打动人的新意。

examples.sentence: The critic dismissed the novel as formulaic because its plot repeated familiar conventions.
examples.translation: 评论家认为这部小说情节重复熟悉套路，因此显得公式化。
examples.note: 文学评论中常评价 plot。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Readers could predict the conclusion before the final scene.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: 强调结果容易猜到，不一定带批评。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The schedule is fixed.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: 强调形式固定，不一定说明缺乏原创。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The character felt stereotyped.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: 强调人物或表达落入刻板模式。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The phrase sounded hackneyed.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: 强调说法或情节用滥了，批评语气更强。

antonyms.example_sentence: The essay offered an original idea.
antonyms.difference_note: 反义，强调有新意和独创性。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The letter uses formal language.
confusable_words.difference_note: formal 和 formulaic 容易靠得很近；formal 侧重“正式的”，formulaic 侧重“公式化的”。

word_family.example_sentence: The team formulated a new plan.

usage_groups.group_title: 公式化的写作、表达或情节
usage_groups.meaning_focus: formulaic 批评表达、故事、写作或方法过于依赖固定模板，因此显得老套、缺少原创性。

examples.translation: 这场演讲听起来很公式化，没能打动观众。
examples.teaching_note: 这里强调演讲像套模板，缺少打动人的新意。

examples.sentence: The critic dismissed the novel as formulaic because its plot repeated familiar conventions.
examples.translation: 评论家认为这部小说情节重复熟悉套路，因此显得公式化。
examples.teaching_note: 文学评论中常评价 plot。

source.source_note: Visual page preview confirms headword and source directions: take over a place/position/role; remove from usual place.

headline.core_cn: 取代；移走；迫使离开原位
headline.core_en: to replace or force someone or something away from its usual place

familiar_bridge.summary: replace 是“换掉”；displace 多了一层“把原来占位的人或东西挤出去”。

core.main: displace 说的不只是换成新的，而是原来在那个位置上的人、居民、工作或理论被挤走。

senses.label: 取代
senses.text: 新技术、理论或制度把旧事物替掉。

senses.label: 迫使离开原处
senses.text: 外力使居民或群体离开原来的地方。

examples.sentence: New machines may displace some factory workers.
examples.translation: 新机器可能会取代一些工厂工人。
examples.note: 这里强调新机器把原有工作位置挤掉。

examples.sentence: The new model gradually displaced the older explanation of the phenomenon.
examples.translation: 这个新模型逐渐取代了对该现象的旧解释。
examples.note: 理论解释也可以被 displace。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Robots may replace some workers.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: 强调替代；displace 还带有把原有对象挤走的感觉。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Please remove the old files.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: 只表示移走，不一定有新事物取代。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The new theory supplanted the old one.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: 更正式，常指后来者逐渐取代原有事物。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The board ousted the director.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: 多指把人从职位或权力中赶走。

antonyms.example_sentence: The company retained its best engineers.
antonyms.difference_note: 反义，强调保留原有人员或事物。

confusable_words.example_sentence: I misplaced my keys.
confusable_words.difference_note: misplace 和 displace 容易靠得很近；misplace 侧重“放错地方”，displace 侧重“取代”。

extension.root_affix: dis- 有“离开、分开”的感觉；place 是“位置”，displace 就是让原本在某处的人或事物离开原位。

word_family.example_sentence: War caused the displacement of many families.

usage_groups.group_title: 把原来占位的人或事物挤走
usage_groups.meaning_focus: displace 不只是 replace；它强调新的力量、技术或理论把原本在位置上的东西挤掉。

examples.sentence: New technology may displace workers in routine jobs.
examples.translation: 新技术可能会取代从事重复性工作的工人。
examples.teaching_note: 这里的重点不是简单换人，而是原来的工作位置被新技术挤掉。

examples.sentence: New machines may displace some factory workers.
examples.translation: 新机器可能会取代一些工厂工人。
examples.teaching_note: 这里强调新机器把原有工作位置挤掉。

usage_groups.student_note: replace 是“换掉”；displace 多了“原来的东西被迫让位”的感觉。

usage_groups.group_title: 迫使人离开原来的地方
usage_groups.meaning_focus: displace 也常指灾害、战争或开发项目迫使居民离开原处。

examples.sentence: A severe flood can displace residents for months.
examples.translation: 严重洪水可能迫使居民离家数月。
examples.teaching_note: 这组用法强调人离开原来的生活地点。

usage_groups.student_note: 这一义项和“取代”共享一个画面：原来的位置被外力改变。

source.source_note: Visual page preview confirms headword and source direction: not able to be reached or achieved.

headline.core_en: impossible or very difficult to reach, achieve, or obtain

familiar_bridge.summary: attain 是“够到、达到”；unattainable 就像目标太高，怎么努力都够不着。

core.main: unattainable 形容目标、标准、理想或东西太难，几乎无法达到或获得。

senses.label: 达不到的；无法获得的
senses.text: impossible or very difficult to reach, achieve, or obtain

examples.sentence: For many beginners, perfect pronunciation may seem unattainable.
examples.translation: 对许多初学者来说，完美发音似乎难以达到。
examples.note: seem unattainable 常用来描述目标看起来太高。

examples.sentence: The reformers argued that perfect social harmony was an unattainable ideal.
examples.translation: 改革者认为完美的社会和谐是无法实现的理想。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The task seemed impossible.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: 最宽泛，表示做不到；unattainable 常指目标太高。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The village was unreachable after the storm.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: 可指物理上到不了，也可指目标达不到。

advanced_synonyms.meaning: 难以得到的；难以捉摸的
advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: A simple solution remained elusive.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: 强调一直追求但难以得到或抓住。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The proposal was infeasible.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: 强调方案在现实条件下不可行。

antonyms.example_sentence: Set an attainable goal.
antonyms.difference_note: 反义，强调目标可以达到。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The document is currently unavailable.
confusable_words.difference_note: unavailable 和 unattainable 容易靠得很近；unavailable 侧重“不可获得的；没空的”，unattainable 侧重“达不到的”。

extension.root_affix: un- 表示否定；attain 表示达到、获得；-able 表示能够。

word_family.example_sentence: Graduation was a major attainment.

usage_groups.group_title: 达不到的目标、标准或理想
usage_groups.meaning_focus: unattainable 形容目标、标准、理想或东西太难，几乎无法达到或获得。

examples.sentence: For many beginners, perfect pronunciation may seem unattainable.
examples.translation: 对许多初学者来说，完美发音似乎难以达到。
examples.teaching_note: seem unattainable 常用来描述目标看起来太高。

examples.sentence: The reformers argued that perfect social harmony was an unattainable ideal.
examples.translation: 改革者认为完美的社会和谐是无法实现的理想。

source.source_note: Visual page preview confirms headword and source phrase: flush with; having plenty of something, especially money.

headline.core_cn: 充裕的；资金或资源充足的
headline.core_en: having plenty of money or resources

familiar_bridge.summary: 最日常的画面是 flush the toilet：冲水。脸红和 flush with resources 也有类似感觉：某种东西一下涌出来、充满起来。

core.main: flush 的底层画面是“冲出、涌上、充满”。正式写作里最重要的是 flush with cash/resources，表示钱或资源一下很充足。

senses.label: 冲洗；冲走
senses.text: flush the toilet 是最直观的用法，用水把东西冲走。

senses.label: 脸红；情绪涌上脸
senses.text: flush with embarrassment 表示因为尴尬、紧张或兴奋而脸红。

senses.label: 充满；资源充足
senses.text: flush with cash/resources/funding 表示资金或资源很充足，是正式阅读里常见且重要的用法。

examples.sentence: Please flush the toilet after using it.
examples.translation: 用完厕所后请冲水。
examples.note: 这个句子给出最直观的“水冲走”的画面。

examples.sentence: She flushed with embarrassment when everyone turned to look at her.
examples.translation: 大家都转头看她时，她尴尬得脸红了。
examples.note: 这里是颜色或情绪一下涌上脸。

examples.sentence: Flush with new funding, the institute hired more researchers.
examples.translation: 有了充足的新资金后，这家机构聘请了更多研究人员。
examples.note: 正式语境重点：flush with new funding 表示资金充足。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The city grew rich through trade.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: 指人、城市或机构富有；flush 更常出现在 flush with cash/resources。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The room was full of light.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: 基础表达，范围很广；flush with 更书面、更有“充满”的画面。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The region has abundant resources.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: 强调资源数量充足，常修饰 resources/supply。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The project is well-funded.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: 只强调资金充足。

antonyms.example_sentence: The team is short of money.
antonyms.difference_note: 反义，强调缺钱或资源不足。

word_family.example_sentence: She began to blush.
Water flows down the street.

focus.title: 正式语境重点
focus.text: flush with cash / resources / funding = 资金或资源很充足。这个结构常出现在公司、机构、项目或政府拿到资金之后。

usage_groups.group_title: 冲洗：水一下子冲走东西
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 最直观的 flush 是用水冲洗、冲走。

examples.sentence: Please flush the toilet after using it.
examples.translation: 用完之后请冲马桶。
examples.teaching_note: 这个画面帮助理解 flush 的底层动作：一股水冲出来，把东西带走。

examples.sentence: Please flush the toilet after using it.
examples.translation: 用完厕所后请冲水。
examples.teaching_note: 这个句子给出最直观的“水冲走”的画面。

usage_groups.student_note: 这个基础画面会延伸到颜色、情绪或资源“涌上来”。

usage_groups.group_title: 脸红：颜色或情绪涌上脸
usage_groups.meaning_focus: flush 可以表示因为尴尬、愤怒或兴奋而脸红。

examples.sentence: She began to flush with embarrassment when everyone turned to look at her.
examples.translation: 大家都看向她时，她尴尬得脸红起来。
examples.teaching_note: 脸红义不是随机记忆；它像颜色一下子涌到脸上。

usage_groups.student_note: 这里的 with 后面接触发脸红的情绪。

usage_groups.group_title: 资源充足：资金或资源像涌上来一样多
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 这里强调钱、资源或机会一下子变得很充足。

examples.sentence: The start-up was suddenly flush with cash after the investment.
examples.translation: 拿到投资后，这家初创公司一下子资金充足。
examples.teaching_note: 正式阅读里常考这一组：flush with 后面接资源，表示手头很宽裕。

usage_groups.student_note: 这个义项不是“数字影响”，也不是抽象拼接；它保留了“涌上来、充满”的画面。

headline.core_en: to hide, cover, or disguise something

familiar_bridge.summary: cloak 像给真相披上一层外衣：别人看到表面，却看不到里面真正的动机。

core.main: cloak 强调用外表、语言或理由把真实意图、事实或情绪遮住。

senses.label: 掩盖；隐藏；伪装
senses.text: to hide, cover, or disguise something

examples.sentence: He cloaked his disappointment with a polite smile.
examples.translation: 他用礼貌的微笑掩饰了自己的失望。
examples.note: cloak 常用于把真实情绪或意图遮住。

examples.sentence: Technical language can cloak political assumptions.
examples.translation: 技术性语言可能会掩盖政治假设。
examples.note: 抽象语境中常用于遮掩观点或假设。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: He tried to hide the truth.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: 最基础，表示把东西藏起来。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Clouds covered the sun.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: 强调遮住表面，不一定有欺骗意图。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The report concealed key facts.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: 更正式，常指隐瞒事实或动机。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: She disguised her disappointment.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: 强调伪装成别的样子。

antonyms.example_sentence: The data revealed a pattern.
antonyms.difference_note: 反义，强调把隐藏的信息显露出来。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The table has a thin coat of paint.
confusable_words.difference_note: coat 和 cloak 容易靠得很近；coat 侧重“外套；涂层”，cloak 侧重“掩盖”。

extension.root_affix: cloak 本义是斗篷；作动词时像“披上一层东西”，所以引申为遮盖、掩饰。

word_family.example_sentence: The proposal was cloaked in technical language.
The investigation helped uncloak the real motive.

related_words.example_sentence: The spy used a disguise to enter the building.

usage_groups.meaning_focus: cloak 强调用外表、语言或理由把真实意图、事实或情绪遮住。

source.source_note: Visual page preview confirms headword and source direction: cause to become unsteady and fall; topple the dictator.

headline.core_en: to make something fall, or remove a leader or government from power

familiar_bridge.summary: topple 先有“站着的东西倒下”的画面；放到政治里，就是政权或领导人被推翻。

core.main: topple 可表示具体地使物体倒下，也可表示推翻领导者、政府或制度。

senses.label: 使倒下
senses.text: 用于 statue, tree 等原本立着的东西。

senses.label: 推翻
senses.text: 用于 dictator, government, regime 等权力对象。

examples.sentence: The strong wind toppled several trees.
examples.translation: 强风吹倒了几棵树。
examples.note: 具体物体倒下。

examples.sentence: The uprising failed to topple the authoritarian regime.
examples.translation: 这场起义未能推翻那个威权政权。
examples.note: 政治语境中常用 topple。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The storm knocked down a tree.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: 可指把具体物体撞倒。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The rebels tried to overthrow the regime.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: 多用于推翻政府、制度或统治者。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The king was deposed.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: 正式用语，强调把统治者或高位者赶下台。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The group tried to subvert the system.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: 强调暗中削弱或颠覆制度。

antonyms.example_sentence: The policy helped stabilize prices.
antonyms.difference_note: 反义，强调使局势或系统稳定下来。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The child tumbled down the hill.
confusable_words.difference_note: tumble 和 topple 容易靠得很近；tumble 侧重“跌倒；滚落”，topple 侧重“推翻”。

usage_groups.group_title: 倒下或推翻原有力量
usage_groups.meaning_focus: topple 可表示具体地使物体倒下，也可表示推翻领导者、政府或制度。

examples.translation: 这场起义未能推翻那个威权政权。
examples.teaching_note: 政治语境中常用 topple。

word_family.example_sentence: The toppled statue became a symbol of the revolt.
The toppling of the regime changed the region.

source.source_note: Visual page preview confirms headword and source directions: understood/appreciated; obtainable/reachable.

headline.core_cn: 可理解的；可接近的；可获得的
headline.core_en: easy to understand, reach, use, or get

familiar_bridge.summary: access 是“进得去、用得上”；accessible 就是门槛低：人能接近，内容也能读懂。

core.main: accessible 的核心是“门槛低”：内容容易懂，或资源、地点、服务容易接近和使用。

senses.label: 容易理解的
senses.text: 用于 explanation, style, book, art 等内容。

senses.label: 可接近、可使用、可获得的
senses.text: 用于 information, service, resource, place 等对象。

examples.sentence: The teacher made the difficult idea accessible to beginners.
examples.translation: 老师把这个难概念讲得让初学者也能理解。
examples.note: 强调容易理解。

examples.sentence: The historian wrote in an accessible style without oversimplifying the evidence.
examples.translation: 这位历史学家用易懂的风格写作，同时没有过度简化证据。
examples.note: 可评价写作风格。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The article is easy to understand.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: 对应“易懂”义，是最基础说法。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The data is available online.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: 强调可获得，不强调是否容易理解。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Her explanation was intelligible.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: 较正式，强调语言或解释能被理解。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The professor is approachable.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: 常指人容易接近，或内容不让人望而却步。

antonyms.example_sentence: The mountain village is inaccessible in winter.
antonyms.difference_note: 反义，可表示地点难到达，也可表示文字难懂。

confusable_words.example_sentence: This answer is acceptable.
confusable_words.difference_note: acceptable 和 accessible 容易靠得很近；acceptable 侧重“可接受的”，accessible 侧重“可理解的”。

extension.root_affix: access 是“进入、使用、获得”；accessible 表示容易进入、使用、理解或获得。

word_family.example_sentence: The mountain village is inaccessible in winter.
The app improved accessibility for users.

usage_groups.group_title: 内容容易理解
usage_groups.meaning_focus: accessible 可以说讲座、书、解释或风格容易懂，门槛低。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 常用来评价内容对初学者或普通读者是否友好。

examples.sentence: The lecture was accessible to beginners.
examples.translation: 这场讲座对初学者来说也容易听懂。
examples.teaching_note: 这里的 accessible 不是“能走到”，而是“能理解”。

examples.sentence: The teacher made the difficult idea accessible to beginners.
examples.translation: 老师把这个难概念讲得让初学者也能理解。
examples.teaching_note: 强调容易理解。

usage_groups.student_note: 这个义项常和 clear、intelligible 一起理解。

usage_groups.group_title: 地点、资源或服务容易到达/获得
usage_groups.meaning_focus: accessible 也可以指地方可到达，资源可使用，信息可获得。

examples.sentence: The village is accessible by train.
examples.translation: 这个村庄可以坐火车到达。
examples.teaching_note: by train 表明它在现实上可到达。

usage_groups.student_note: 两个义项共享“能接触到”的核心，只是一个是理解，一个是到达或使用。

source.source_note: Visual page preview confirms headword and source direction: functioning as a means; instrumental in bringing about negotiations.

headline.core_cn: 起重要作用的；有帮助的
headline.core_en: playing an important role in making something happen

familiar_bridge.summary: instrument 是“工具”；instrumental 说的是某个人或因素像关键工具一样，把结果推出来。

core.main: instrumental 强调某人、因素或行动对结果起关键促成作用，不只是一般有帮助。

senses.label: 起重要作用的；有帮助的
senses.text: playing an important role in making something happen

examples.sentence: Her advice was instrumental in helping me choose the right program.
examples.translation: 她的建议对我选择合适项目起了重要作用。
examples.note: instrumental 比 helpful 更强，表示建议真正促成了结果。

examples.sentence: The discovery was instrumental in reshaping scientists' understanding of early human migration.
examples.translation: 这一发现对重塑科学家关于早期人类迁徙的理解起了重要作用。
examples.note: 可用于发现推动理论变化。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Her advice was helpful.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: 较弱，只表示有帮助。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Sleep is important for memory.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: 范围宽，未必说明它直接促成结果。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Timing was crucial.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: 强调关键、不可或缺。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The discovery was pivotal.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: 强调在转折或结果形成中处在枢纽位置。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The cafe played instrumental music.
confusable_words.difference_note: instrumental music 和 instrumental 容易靠得很近；instrumental music 侧重“器乐”，instrumental 侧重“起重要作用的”。

extension.root_affix: instrument 是“工具”；instrumental 抽象成“起工具性作用、关键作用”。

headline.core_cn: 被错误判断引导的；方向错误的
headline.core_en: based on a wrong idea or poor judgment

familiar_bridge.summary: guide 是“引导”；misguided 就像判断被带错方向，动机可能不坏，但方法或前提错了。

core.main: misguided 常批评政策、努力、信念或尝试建立在错误判断上；它强调方向错，不一定强调恶意。

senses.label: 方向错误的判断或行动
senses.text: based on poor judgment or a mistaken idea

examples.sentence: The charity made a misguided attempt to help without asking what the community actually needed.
examples.translation: 这个慈善组织没有先了解社区真正需要什么，就做了一次方向错误的帮助尝试。
examples.note: misguided attempt 常表示动机未必坏，但判断错了。

examples.sentence: The author regards the reform as misguided because it treats a symptom rather than the cause.
examples.translation: 作者认为这项改革方向错误，因为它处理的是症状而不是根源。
examples.note: 阅读中常用来批评方案的基本思路。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: It was an unwise decision.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: 更日常，强调决定不聪明或不稳妥。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The proposal was wrongheaded from the start.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: 语气更强，强调思路从一开始就错。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The plan was ill-conceived.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: 常形容 plan/project/policy 设计得不好。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The conclusion was erroneous.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: 更偏事实或结论错误，不一定涉及好意。

antonyms.example_sentence: A well-advised response begins with evidence.
antonyms.difference_note: 反义，强调经过良好判断或建议。

confusable_words.example_sentence: A misleading graph gives readers the wrong impression.
confusable_words.difference_note: misleading 是“会误导别人”；misguided 是“自己判断被带错”。

extension.root_affix: mis- 常表示“错误、不当”；guided 是“被引导的”，misguided 就是被错误想法引导。

word_family.example_sentence: The headline may mislead readers.
The policy resulted from years of misguidance.

usage_groups.group_title: 好意或计划被错误判断带偏
usage_groups.meaning_focus: misguided 强调判断或出发点本身错了，因此行动看似有目的，却走向了错误方向。

examples.sentence: The charity made a misguided attempt to help without asking what the community actually needed.
examples.translation: 这个慈善组织没有先了解社区真正需要什么，就做了一次方向错误的帮助尝试。
examples.teaching_note: misguided attempt 常表示动机未必坏，但判断错了。

examples.sentence: The author regards the reform as misguided because it treats a symptom rather than the cause.
examples.translation: 作者认为这项改革方向错误，因为它处理的是症状而不是根源。
examples.teaching_note: 阅读中常用来批评方案的基本思路。

source.source_meaning_cn: 搞 塞 的 ， 推 透 的； 闪烁 其 词 的 tending

headline.core_en: avoiding a direct answer, action, or capture

familiar_bridge.summary: avoid 是“避开”；evasive 更像别人问到关键处时，故意绕开重点。

core.main: evasive 最常形容回答、语言或态度不正面；也可形容躲避危险的动作。GRE 阅读中重点通常是“闪烁其词、回避问题”。

senses.label: 回答回避
senses.text: avoiding a direct answer

senses.label: 动作躲避
senses.text: done to avoid danger or capture

examples.sentence: The spokesperson gave an evasive answer and never explained where the money went.
examples.translation: 发言人回答得闪烁其词，始终没有解释钱去了哪里。
examples.note: answer 是最常见搭配之一。

examples.sentence: The official’s evasive language suggests that the policy has weaknesses he does not want to admit.
examples.translation: 这位官员回避性的措辞暗示政策有他不愿承认的弱点。
examples.note: 阅读中可据此判断人物态度或隐瞒。

examples.sentence: The driver took evasive action to avoid the deer.
examples.translation: 司机采取躲避动作以避开那只鹿。
examples.note: 这个义项更偏动作，不是说话含糊。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: His answer was indirect.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: 语气较弱，可能只是表达委婉。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The child became avoidant after repeated criticism.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: 更偏心理或行为倾向。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Her statement was deliberately equivocal.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: 强调措辞故意保留多种解释。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The minister gave a noncommittal reply.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: 强调不承诺、不明确表态。

antonyms.example_sentence: A direct answer would end the confusion.
antonyms.difference_note: 反义，强调正面回答。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The cause of the disease remained elusive.
confusable_words.difference_note: elusive 是对象难找；evasive 是人或回答在躲避。

extension.root_affix: evade 是“逃避、躲开”；evasive 是它的形容词。

word_family.example_sentence: He tried to evade responsibility.
Tax evasion is illegal.

usage_groups.group_title: 回答或措辞回避关键问题
usage_groups.meaning_focus: evasive 强调故意绕开重点，让别人无法得到明确回答。

examples.sentence: The spokesperson gave an evasive answer and never explained where the money went.
examples.translation: 发言人回答得闪烁其词，始终没有解释钱去了哪里。
examples.teaching_note: answer 是最常见搭配之一。

examples.sentence: The official’s evasive language suggests that the policy has weaknesses he does not want to admit.
examples.translation: 这位官员回避性的措辞暗示政策有他不愿承认的弱点。
examples.teaching_note: 阅读中可据此判断人物态度或隐瞒。

usage_groups.group_title: 为避开危险而躲闪
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 这里强调迅速避开危险或攻击，而不是语言上的含糊。

examples.sentence: The driver took evasive action to avoid the deer.
examples.translation: 司机采取躲避动作以避开那只鹿。
examples.teaching_note: 这个义项更偏动作，不是说话含糊。

source.source_meaning_cn: 和 赁 直觉 的； 本 能 的

headline.core_en: understood or done by instinct rather than step-by-step reasoning

familiar_bridge.summary: intuition 是“直觉”；intuitive 强调不用一步步推理，也能自然感觉到或用明白。

core.main: intuitive 可以形容判断来自直觉，也可以形容设计、解释或界面很直观、容易上手。它不是 irrational，直觉有时也可以很准确。

senses.label: 凭直觉的
senses.text: based on instinctive understanding

senses.label: 直观易用的
senses.text: easy to understand or use without explanation

examples.sentence: She had an intuitive sense that the explanation was incomplete.
examples.translation: 她凭直觉感觉这个解释并不完整。
examples.note: 这里强调先于完整推理的判断。

examples.sentence: The app has an intuitive interface, so new users can find the main tools quickly.
examples.translation: 这个应用界面很直观，新用户很快就能找到主要工具。
examples.note: interface 是现代英语中常见搭配。

examples.sentence: Although the theory is mathematically complex, its central idea is intuitive.
examples.translation: 虽然这个理论在数学上很复杂，但它的核心想法很直观。
examples.note: 学术语境中可表示“核心逻辑容易感觉到”。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Her reaction was instinctive.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: 更强调身体或心理的本能反应。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The gesture felt natural.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: 更宽泛，可形容不费力、不生硬。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The claim seems self-evident.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: 强调一看就明白，不一定来自直觉。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The solution is commonsensical.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: 强调符合常识判断。

antonyms.example_sentence: The result is counterintuitive.
antonyms.difference_note: 直接反义，表示和直觉预期相反。

confusable_words.example_sentence: An intuitive answer is not necessarily irrational.
confusable_words.difference_note: intuitive 是直觉式理解；irrational 是缺少理性依据。

extension.root_affix: intuition 是“直觉”；intuitive 是形容词，counterintuitive 表示“反直觉的”。

word_family.example_sentence: Her intuition was right.
The finding was counterintuitive.

usage_groups.group_title: 凭直觉作出的判断或反应
usage_groups.meaning_focus: intuitive 强调不用一步步推理，也能马上感觉到或判断出来。

examples.sentence: She had an intuitive sense that the explanation was incomplete.
examples.translation: 她凭直觉感觉这个解释并不完整。
examples.teaching_note: 这里强调先于完整推理的判断。

usage_groups.group_title: 界面、解释或想法直观易懂
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 这里强调设计或说明让人一看就会用、一听就明白。

examples.sentence: The app has an intuitive interface, so new users can find the main tools quickly.
examples.translation: 这个应用界面很直观，新用户很快就能找到主要工具。
examples.teaching_note: interface 是现代英语中常见搭配。

examples.sentence: Although the theory is mathematically complex, its central idea is intuitive.
examples.translation: 虽然这个理论在数学上很复杂，但它的核心想法很直观。
examples.teaching_note: 学术语境中可表示“核心逻辑容易感觉到”。

source.source_note: From archived Unit 1 OCR extraction; source text used only as a headword/page/order clue.
source.source_meaning_cn: 精明 的； ( 商业 等 方面 ) 有 了 眼光 的

headline.core_en: clever, careful, and good at judging practical advantage

familiar_bridge.summary: smart 是聪明；canny 更像在商业、谈判或判断中懂得抓机会、避风险。

core.main: canny 带有实用智慧：看得准、算得清、不会轻易吃亏。它通常是褒义，但也可能有一点“太会盘算”的感觉。

senses.label: 精明的；会盘算的
senses.text: clever and practically wise

examples.sentence: The canny investor sold the stock before the market fell.
examples.translation: 这位精明的投资者在市场下跌前卖出了股票。
examples.note: canny investor 强调实用判断力。

examples.sentence: The author describes the politician’s compromise as a canny move rather than a sign of weakness.
examples.translation: 作者把这位政治家的妥协描述为精明之举，而不是软弱的表现。
examples.note: 阅读中常评价策略判断。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: That was a clever move.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: 范围更宽，不一定强调实际利益判断。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: She made a shrewd decision.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: 和 canny 很近，常强调现实判断力。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The analysis was astute.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: 更正式，强调洞察力。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: A sagacious leader avoids needless risks.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: 更书面，强调深远判断。

antonyms.example_sentence: A naive investor may follow every rumor.
antonyms.difference_note: 反义，强调缺乏现实判断。

confusable_words.example_sentence: Cunning often suggests deception; canny can be approving.
confusable_words.difference_note: cunning 常带欺骗色彩；canny 可以是赞赏。

extension.root_affix: canny 表示精明、有现实判断力；uncanny 不是它的简单反义，而是“诡异的”。

word_family.example_sentence: Her canniness helped her avoid the risky deal.
The robot’s humanlike face looked uncanny.

related_words.example_sentence: A shrewd investor noticed the pattern early.

usage_groups.group_title: 精明地判断利益和风险
usage_groups.meaning_focus: canny 带有精明、会盘算的感觉，重点是能判断局势并抓住有利机会。

examples.sentence: The canny investor sold the stock before the market fell.
examples.translation: 这位精明的投资者在市场下跌前卖出了股票。
examples.teaching_note: canny investor 强调实用判断力。

examples.sentence: The author describes the politician’s compromise as a canny move rather than a sign of weakness.
examples.translation: 作者把这位政治家的妥协描述为精明之举，而不是软弱的表现。
examples.teaching_note: 阅读中常评价策略判断。

source.source_note: From archived Unit 1 OCR extraction; source text used only as a headword/page/order clue.
source.source_meaning_cn: 可 理解 的； 易 懂 的

headline.core_en: able to be understood

familiar_bridge.summary: comprehend 是“理解”；comprehensible 是“能被理解”。

core.main: comprehensible 不等于 easy。一个内容可以很难，但只要结构清楚、解释充分，仍然是 comprehensible。

senses.label: 可理解的
senses.text: able to be understood

examples.sentence: The teacher turned a difficult theory into a comprehensible explanation.
examples.translation: 老师把一个困难理论讲成了可理解的解释。
examples.note: 强调解释能被听懂。

examples.sentence: The report is technical, but its main argument remains comprehensible.
examples.translation: 这份报告很技术化，但它的主要论点仍然可理解。
examples.note: 可用于正式文本评价。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: His explanation was understandable.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: 更日常，强调能听懂或看懂。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The instructions were clear.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: 强调表达清楚，不一定强调理解过程。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The speech was barely intelligible.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: 更正式，常指语言或声音能被辨认理解。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Her prose is lucid.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: 强调表达清楚、有条理。

antonyms.example_sentence: The message was incomprehensible.
antonyms.difference_note: 直接反义。

confusable_words.example_sentence: A comprehensive report is not always comprehensible.
confusable_words.difference_note: comprehensive 是覆盖全面；comprehensible 是容易理解。

extension.root_affix: comprehend 是“理解”；-ible 表示“能够被……的”。

word_family.example_sentence: Few readers fully comprehend the theory.
Reading comprehension requires context.

usage_groups.group_title: 解释、语言或说明能被理解

headline.core_en: including all or nearly all important parts

familiar_bridge.summary: complete 是完整；comprehensive 更强调覆盖面广，重要方面都照顾到。

core.main: comprehensive 评价研究、计划、综述或调查覆盖范围广，包含主要方面。它不是“容易懂”，而是“内容全面”。

senses.label: 全面的；综合的
senses.text: covering all or nearly all important parts

examples.sentence: The committee issued a comprehensive report on the causes of the crisis.

examples.note: comprehensive report 强调覆盖范围广。

examples.sentence: The reform requires a comprehensive plan rather than a series of isolated measures.
examples.translation: 这项改革需要一个综合计划，而不是一系列孤立措施。
examples.note: 常用来评价方案是否整体。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The list is complete.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: 强调没有缺漏，语气比 comprehensive 更简单。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The course offers a broad introduction.

advanced_synonyms.difference_note: 比 comprehensive 更强调彻底、不遗漏。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The investigation was thorough.

antonyms.example_sentence: The evidence gives only a partial picture.
antonyms.difference_note: 反义，强调只覆盖一部分。

extension.root_affix: comprehensive 保留 comprehend 里“包含、把握整体”的方向，重点是覆盖全面。

headline.core_cn: 公众注意的焦点；使受到关注
headline.core_en: public attention, or to focus attention on something

familiar_bridge.summary: spotlight 本来是舞台上的聚光灯；灯打到哪里，大家就看哪里。

core.main: spotlight 作名词是公众注意的焦点；作动词是让某个问题、人物或趋势被看见。

senses.label: 公众注意的焦点
senses.text: public attention focused on someone or something

senses.label: 使受到关注
senses.text: to focus attention on something

examples.sentence: The scandal put the company in the spotlight.
examples.translation: 这桩丑闻让这家公司处在公众关注之下。

examples.sentence: The report spotlighted the hidden costs of the reform.

examples.note: 报告、文章、研究都可以 spotlight 问题。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The case drew public attention.

headline.core_en: to cause something to start; something that starts a reaction

familiar_bridge.summary: trigger 本来是枪的扳机；一扣下去，后面的反应就开始了。

core.main: trigger 强调某事成为启动点，引发反应、争论、危机、记忆或变化。它不一定是唯一原因，但常是直接导火索。

senses.label: 引发；触发
senses.text: to cause a reaction, event, or process to begin

senses.label: 导火索
senses.text: something that starts a reaction

examples.translation: 这项公告在网上引发了激烈争论。
examples.note: trigger a debate 很自然。

examples.sentence: A minor environmental change can trigger a major shift in animal behavior.
examples.translation: 一个小的环境变化可能引发动物行为的重大转变。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The storm caused delays.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: cause 范围更广，可指根本原因；trigger 常指直接启动点。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The speech started a debate.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The crisis precipitated reform.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: 更正式，常指使事情突然发生。

antonyms.example_sentence: Early action can prevent a crisis.
antonyms.difference_note: 反义，阻止事情发生。

source.source_meaning_cn: PA；具有 ( 特征、 标志 等 )

headline.core_en: to carry, have, show, or endure something

familiar_bridge.summary: bear 的底层感觉是“承载”：可以承载重量，也可以承载责任、痕迹、相似性或压力。

core.main: 本单元重点是动词 bear：bear a mark / bear resemblance 表示带有特征，bear responsibility / cost 表示承担。看到 bear，要看它后面承载的对象。

senses.label: 带有某种特征
senses.text: to have or show a mark, sign, or resemblance

senses.label: 承担或忍受
senses.text: to carry a responsibility, cost, burden, or pain

examples.sentence: The document bears the mark of careful editing.
examples.translation: 这份文件带有细致编辑的痕迹。
examples.note: bear a mark 表示“带有某种标记”。

examples.sentence: Consumers, not companies, ultimately bear the cost of the policy.
examples.translation: 最终承担这项政策成本的是消费者，而不是公司。
examples.note: bear the cost 是正式阅读中常见用法。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The wall carries the weight.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: 最接近 bear 的具体承载义。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The plan has several risks.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: 更普通，缺少 bear 的正式感和承载感。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The sample exhibits unusual features.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: 常用于正式描述“呈现特征”。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The bridge can withstand strong winds.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: 只对应“承受住压力或冲击”的义项。

antonyms.example_sentence: The company tried to avoid the cost.
antonyms.difference_note: 在 bear responsibility/cost 语境中形成反义。

confusable_words.example_sentence: Bare and bear sound the same but mean different things.
confusable_words.difference_note: bare 是形容词“裸露的”；bear 是动词“承载/承担”。

extension.root_affix: bear 的核心画面是“承载”；bearing、bearable 都保留了这个方向。

word_family.example_sentence: The evidence has a bearing on the case.
The pain was bearable.

source.source_meaning_cn: 举止； 风度

headline.core_en: a person’s manner, or relevance to something

familiar_bridge.summary: bear 是“承载”；bearing 可以是一个人身上带出来的姿态，也可以是证据对问题“承载”的关系。

core.main: bearing 有两个常见方向：人的举止风度；have a bearing on 表示“与……有关、对……有影响”。

senses.label: 举止；风度
senses.text: a person’s manner or way of carrying themselves

senses.label: 关系；影响
senses.text: relevance or influence, especially in have a bearing on

examples.sentence: Despite the pressure, she maintained a calm bearing.
examples.translation: 尽管压力很大，她仍保持镇定的风度。
examples.note: bearing 可写人的姿态。

examples.sentence: The new evidence has a direct bearing on the debate.
examples.translation: 新证据与这场争论有直接关系。
examples.note: have a bearing on 是重点结构。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: His manner was polite.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: 对应人的举止义，语气更日常。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The detail has little relevance.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: 对应 have a bearing on 的相关性义。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Her demeanor remained calm.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: 更正式，常写人的外在表现。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The pertinence of the evidence is disputed.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: 更书面，强调证据或论点是否切题。

antonyms.example_sentence: The example’s irrelevance weakens the argument.
antonyms.difference_note: 反义，强调与讨论对象无关。

extension.root_affix: bearing 来自 bear 的“承载”感：人承载出的姿态，或证据承载出的关联。

word_family.example_sentence: Consumers may bear the cost.
The delay was bearable.

usage_groups.group_title: 人的举止或风度
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 这里指一个人表现出来的姿态、气度或举止。

examples.sentence: Despite the pressure, she maintained a calm bearing.

source.source_meaning_cn: 生育

headline.core_en: the process or period of giving birth to children

familiar_bridge.summary: bear 有一个较正式的旧义是“生育”；childbearing 就是围绕“生孩子”这个阶段、能力或选择展开。

core.main: childbearing 是正式中性词，常用于医学、人口、社会政策语境，讨论生育年龄、生育选择或生育模式。

examples.sentence: The study followed women throughout their childbearing years.
examples.translation: 这项研究追踪了女性整个育龄阶段。
examples.note: childbearing years 是固定说法。

examples.sentence: The passage links changes in childbearing patterns to broader economic pressures.
examples.translation: 这段文字将生育模式的变化和更广泛的经济压力联系起来。
examples.note: 阅读中常用于社会趋势分析。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Reproduction is affected by both biology and social conditions.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: reproduction 更偏生物学或总体繁殖过程；childbearing 更具体指人生中的生育孩子。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Fertility rates declined in several regions.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: fertility 强调能不能生育或生育率；childbearing 强调生育行为、阶段或选择。

antonyms.example_sentence: The study compared childbearing with voluntary childlessness.
antonyms.difference_note: childlessness 是没有子女的状态，和 childbearing 形成对照。

confusable_words.example_sentence: Childbearing may take months, but childrearing takes years.
confusable_words.difference_note: childbearing 是生育；childrearing 是把孩子养大。

extension.root_affix: child + bearing：bearing 在这里来自 bear 的“生育”义。

word_family.example_sentence: Childrearing continues long after birth.
The birthrate fell sharply after the crisis.

related_words.example_sentence: bear children 是较正式的“生育孩子”。

usage_groups.group_title: 生育年龄、选择或社会趋势
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 这里关注生育作为人生阶段、身体能力或社会现象。

examples.sentence: The study followed women throughout their childbearing years.
examples.translation: 这项研究追踪了女性整个育龄阶段。
examples.teaching_note: childbearing years 指可能生育的年龄阶段。

examples.sentence: Economic pressure can influence childbearing decisions.
examples.translation: 经济压力会影响生育决定。
examples.teaching_note: 正式语境中常把 childbearing 和社会、经济因素联系起来。

headline.core_en: the process or period of having children

source.source_meaning_cn: 严厉 批评； 责骂 rebuke or reprimand severely: He chastised Congress for cutting his

headline.core_en: to criticize someone severely

familiar_bridge.summary: criticize 是一般批评；chastise 的语气更重，像公开或带权威地责备。

core.main: chastise 强调严厉指责，通常因为对方做错事、失职或违反规范。

examples.sentence: The coach chastised the players for ignoring the basic rules.
examples.translation: 教练严厉批评队员无视基本规则。
examples.note: for 后面说明责备原因。

examples.sentence: The editorial chastises the government for cutting funds to public schools.
examples.translation: 这篇社论严厉批评政府削减公立学校经费。
examples.note: 正式写作中可用于媒体或作者态度。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The teacher scolded the class for talking during the exam.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: scold 更日常，常用于老师、父母等直接责骂。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Critics criticized the plan for ignoring local needs.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: criticize 范围更广，可轻可重；chastise 更严厉。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The judge rebuked the lawyer for interrupting the witness.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: rebuke 更正式，强调权威性的严厉批评。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The officer was reprimanded for violating procedure.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: reprimand 常带官方或制度性的处分意味。

antonyms.example_sentence: The mayor praised the volunteers for their quick response.
antonyms.difference_note: praise 是正面评价，与 chastise 的责备方向相反。

extension.root_affix: chastise 可和 chasten 联系：都含有通过批评或经历让人收敛、受教训的感觉。

word_family.example_sentence: The speech sounded like a public chastisement.
The team returned chastened after the defeat.

usage_groups.group_title: 因为错误或失职而严厉责备
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 重点不是提出意见，而是带着明显不满和责备语气。

examples.sentence: The coach chastised the players for ignoring the basic rules.
examples.translation: 教练严厉批评队员无视基本规则。
examples.teaching_note: for 后面常说明责备的原因。

examples.sentence: The editorial chastised the government for cutting funds to public schools.
examples.translation: 这篇社论严厉批评政府削减公立学校经费。
examples.teaching_note: 可用于媒体、作者或权威机构的强烈批评。

source.source_meaning_cn: flit；et lack of courage or confidence: overcome one’s timidity 克服 胆 性 | His timidity prevented him from asking her

headline.core_en: lack of courage or confidence

familiar_bridge.summary: shy 是害羞；timidity 更强调因为胆怯而不敢说、不敢做。

core.main: timidity 描述缺少勇气或自信，结果是回避行动、表达或风险。

examples.sentence: His timidity kept him from asking the question he most wanted to ask.
examples.translation: 他的胆怯让他没能问出最想问的问题。
examples.note: 体现“不敢行动”。

examples.sentence: The author criticizes the committee's timidity in the face of obvious injustice.
examples.translation: 作者批评委员会面对明显不公时的怯懦。
examples.note: 可用于批评机构缺乏勇气。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Her shyness made conversation difficult.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: shyness 多指社交害羞；timidity 更强调胆怯导致退缩。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Fearfulness limited his choices.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: fearfulness 更直接指害怕；timidity 常带性格或态度上的怯懦。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: His diffidence hid real talent.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: diffidence 更强调不自信和不敢表现自己。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Faintheartedness delayed reform.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: faintheartedness 语气更批评，指缺少勇气。

antonyms.example_sentence: Boldness helped her speak up in the meeting.
antonyms.difference_note: boldness 强调敢说敢做，是 timidity 的直接对照。

antonyms.example_sentence: Confidence made the speaker sound calm.
antonyms.difference_note: confidence 侧重相信自己能做到。

confusable_words.example_sentence: Humility can coexist with courage; timidity cannot.
confusable_words.difference_note: humility 是不自大，可能是优点；timidity 是不敢行动。

extension.root_affix: timid 是“胆怯的”；timidity 是名词形式。

word_family.example_sentence: A timid child may avoid speaking in class.
The threat intimidated witnesses.

usage_groups.group_title: 因为胆怯而不敢行动
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 这里关注胆怯造成的退缩，而不是单纯安静或礼貌。

examples.sentence: His timidity kept him from asking the question he most wanted to ask.
examples.translation: 他的胆怯让他没能问出最想问的问题。
examples.teaching_note: 体现“不敢行动”的结果。

examples.sentence: The author criticizes the committee's timidity in the face of obvious injustice.
examples.translation: 作者批评委员会面对明显不公时的怯懦。
examples.teaching_note: 可用于批评个人或机构缺乏勇气。

source.source_meaning_cn: 威 胁； 恐吓； 吓 距 frighten or overawe (Someone), especially in order

headline.core_en: to frighten someone so that they do what you want or stop acting

familiar_bridge.summary: timid 是胆怯；intimidate 是让别人胆怯到不敢行动。

core.main: intimidate 不只是吓到别人，通常带有目的：用威胁、权力或压力让对方退缩。

examples.sentence: The manager tried to intimidate employees into staying silent.
examples.translation: 经理试图恐吓员工，让他们保持沉默。
examples.note: into doing 表示逼迫对方做某事。

examples.sentence: The passage describes how powerful groups intimidate witnesses and weaken legal accountability.
examples.translation: 文章描述强势群体如何恐吓证人，从而削弱法律问责。
examples.note: 正式语境中常用于权力压制。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The sudden noise frightened the child.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: frighten 只是使害怕；intimidate 常含“用害怕来控制”。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: They threatened the witness before the trial.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: threaten 强调发出威胁；intimidate 强调威胁造成的压制效果。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The regime coerced citizens into obedience.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: coerce 更强调通过压力迫使服从。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: He bullied younger students into giving him money.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: bully 常指长期或仗势欺人。

antonyms.example_sentence: The doctor reassured the patient before the operation.
antonyms.difference_note: reassure 是减少恐惧，方向与 intimidate 相反。

confusable_words.example_sentence: A timid witness may be easy to intimidate.
confusable_words.difference_note: timid 描述害怕的人；intimidate 描述造成害怕的动作。

extension.root_affix: 和 timid 同源；in- 在这里不是简单否定，而是构成“使人胆怯”的动词。

word_family.example_sentence: Voter intimidation is illegal.
The professor had an intimidating presence.

related_words.example_sentence: timid 是被恐惧压住的状态。

usage_groups.group_title: 用压力或威胁迫使别人退缩
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 重点是制造恐惧，从而影响对方的选择或行动。

examples.sentence: The manager tried to intimidate employees into staying silent.
examples.translation: 经理试图恐吓员工，让他们保持沉默。
examples.teaching_note: into doing 表示逼迫对方做某事。

examples.sentence: Powerful groups may intimidate witnesses and weaken legal accountability.
examples.translation: 强势群体可能恐吓证人，从而削弱法律问责。
examples.teaching_note: 正式语境中常用于权力压制。

headline.core_en: to frighten or pressure someone into doing or not doing something

source.source_meaning_cn: 沙文 主义 的； 过 分 偏 祖 自 己 所 属 群体 的 displaying excessive or prejudiced

headline.core_cn: 盲目优越的；沙文主义的
headline.core_en: showing excessive or prejudiced loyalty to one's own group

familiar_bridge.summary: pride 是自豪；chauvinistic 是过分相信自己的国家、性别或群体更优越。

core.main: chauvinistic 带明显负面评价，指狭隘的群体优越感和对外部群体的偏见。

examples.sentence: His chauvinistic remarks suggested that only people from his country could be truly civilized.
examples.translation: 他的沙文主义言论暗示只有他国家的人才算真正文明。
examples.note: 体现“自己群体优越”的偏见。

examples.sentence: The passage criticizes the movement's chauvinistic view of culture and identity.
examples.translation: 文章批评这一运动对文化和身份的沙文主义看法。
examples.note: 阅读中常用于作者负面评价。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The policy reflected a prejudiced view of immigrants.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: prejudiced 更宽泛，指先入为主的偏见。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The report was biased against rural communities.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: biased 强调判断不公正；chauvinistic 更强调群体优越感。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The speech sounded jingoistic and hostile to outsiders.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: jingoistic 主要指激进、好斗的民族主义。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The policy was criticized as xenophobic.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: xenophobic 强调害怕或敌视外国人。

antonyms.example_sentence: An open-minded reader considers unfamiliar views seriously.
antonyms.difference_note: open-minded 强调愿意接受不同观点。

antonyms.example_sentence: The reform reflected egalitarian values.
antonyms.difference_note: egalitarian 强调平等，与优越感相反。

confusable_words.example_sentence: A patriotic speech need not be chauvinistic.
confusable_words.difference_note: patriotic 可以是正面的爱国；chauvinistic 是过度且带偏见的优越感。

usage_groups.group_title: 带有群体优越感和偏见
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 它不是普通喜爱自己的群体，而是把自己群体看得更高、把别人看低。

examples.sentence: His chauvinistic remarks suggested that only people from his country could be truly civilized.
examples.translation: 他的沙文主义言论暗示只有他国家的人才算真正文明。
examples.teaching_note: 体现“自己群体优越”的偏见。

examples.sentence: The passage criticizes the movement's chauvinistic view of culture and identity.
examples.translation: 文章批评这一运动对文化和身份的沙文主义看法。
examples.teaching_note: 阅读中常用于作者负面评价。

extension.root_affix: 来自法语人名 Chauvin，后来表示盲目、过度的群体忠诚。

word_family.example_sentence: The essay attacks cultural chauvinism.
The critic called him a chauvinist.

related_words.example_sentence: male chauvinism 指男性优越和性别偏见。

headline.core_cn: 沙文主义的；盲目优越的
headline.core_en: showing excessive loyalty to one group and prejudice against others

source.source_meaning_cn: S48 ( 知识 或 能 力 )； 精通 acquire

headline.core_en: to learn something so well that one can use it skillfully

familiar_bridge.summary: learn 是学会；master 是熟到能控制、运用、解决问题。

core.main: master 强调真正掌握，不只是知道概念，而是能熟练运用技能、方法或知识。

examples.sentence: It takes years to master a foreign language.
examples.translation: 掌握一门外语需要多年时间。
examples.note: master 比 learn 程度更高。

examples.sentence: The passage argues that scientists must master both theory and measurement before interpreting the data.
examples.translation: 文章认为科学家在解释数据之前，必须同时掌握理论和测量方法。
examples.note: 学术语境中强调熟练控制工具或知识。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Children learn new words quickly.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: learn 只表示学习过程；master 表示已经学到熟练。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: She understands the rule but cannot apply it yet.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: understand 是明白；master 还要能运用。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: He commands several languages.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: command 强调熟练支配和运用。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Students assimilate new concepts gradually.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: assimilate 强调把新知识吸收进自己的理解体系。

antonyms.example_sentence: He fumbled with the new software.
antonyms.difference_note: fumble 表示不熟练、手忙脚乱。

confusable_words.example_sentence: She memorized the formula but had not mastered the method.
confusable_words.difference_note: memorize 是记住；master 是能灵活使用。

extension.root_affix: master 作名词可指“大师、主人”；作动词时发展出“掌握、驾驭”。

word_family.example_sentence: Mastery requires practice.
The performance was masterful.
The novel is considered a masterpiece.

usage_groups.group_title: 把技能或知识学到能熟练运用
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 重点是从理解走到熟练控制。

examples.sentence: It takes years to master a foreign language.

headline.core_en: loud, noisy, and demanding attention

headline.core_cn: 理智的；偏智性的；大脑的
headline.core_en: intellectual rather than emotional; related to the brain

familiar_bridge.summary: cerebral 和 brain 有关；形容作品或风格时，表示偏理性、用脑多、情绪少。

core.main: cerebral 有两个常见方向：评价作品或思维偏智性；医学中表示和大脑有关。

examples.sentence: The film is cerebral rather than sentimental.
examples.translation: 这部电影偏理智，而不是煽情。
examples.note: 评论语境中常与 emotional/sentimental 对照。

examples.sentence: The passage describes the philosopher's prose as cerebral, demanding patient analysis rather than quick emotional response.
examples.translation: 这段文字将这位哲学家的文字描述为偏智性的，需要耐心分析而不是快速情绪反应。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The debate was intellectual rather than emotional.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: intellectual 更普通，强调思考和知识。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The approach is analytical and careful.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: analytical 强调拆解和分析问题。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The essay is ratiocinative rather than lyrical.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: ratiocinative 更书面，强调逻辑推理。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The lecture was abstract but rigorous.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: abstract 强调概念层面，不一定像 cerebral 那样带智性评价。

antonyms.example_sentence: The appeal was emotional rather than analytical.
antonyms.difference_note: emotional 强调情感反应。

antonyms.example_sentence: The scene was sentimental but sincere.
antonyms.difference_note: sentimental 更偏感伤或煽情。

confusable_words.difference_note: cerebellar 是“小脑的”，是医学细分词；cerebral 更广泛指“大脑的”或“智性的”。

extension.root_affix: cerebr- 和“大脑”有关。

examples.teaching_note: cerebral prose 强调文字需要理性处理。

headline.core_en: treating people unfairly because of identity or group

familiar_bridge.summary: discriminate 原本可以指“区分”；discriminatory 在现代语境中通常是负面的“歧视性”。

core.main: discriminatory 描述政策、规则或做法按身份、群体或背景不公平地区别对待。

examples.sentence: The court ruled that the hiring rule was discriminatory.
examples.translation: 法院裁定这项招聘规则具有歧视性。
examples.note: rule/policy 常见。

examples.sentence: The passage argues that seemingly neutral standards can have discriminatory effects.
examples.translation: 文章认为，看似中立的标准也可能产生歧视性影响。
examples.note: 阅读中常讨论制度性不公平。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The rule was unfair to applicants from poor families.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: unfair 很普通；discriminatory 更具体指基于身份或群体的不公平。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The test was biased against nonnative speakers.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: biased 强调判断或设计偏向一方。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The policy was prejudicial to minority groups.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: prejudicial 强调偏见造成不利影响。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The rule was exclusionary in practice.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: exclusionary 强调把某些人排除在外。

antonyms.example_sentence: An impartial judge treats both sides fairly.
antonyms.difference_note: impartial 强调不偏向任何一方。

antonyms.example_sentence: The new policy created a more equitable system.
antonyms.difference_note: equitable 强调结果或制度更公平。

confusable_words.example_sentence: A discriminating reader notices nuance.
confusable_words.difference_note: discriminating 可以是正面的“有鉴别力”；discriminatory 是负面的“歧视性”。

extension.root_affix: discriminate 的核心是“区分”；discriminatory 是带负面色彩的“歧视性区分”。

word_family.example_sentence: The law bans discrimination.

headline.core_en: able to judge quality and notice fine differences

familiar_bridge.summary: taste 是品味；discriminating 是能分辨细微好坏，有判断力。

core.main: discriminating 在这里是正面的“有鉴别力”，不要和 discriminatory 的负面“歧视性”混淆。

examples.sentence: A discriminating reader notices the difference between elegance and mere ornament.
examples.translation: 有鉴别力的读者能看出优雅和单纯装饰之间的区别。
examples.note: reader 搭配自然。

examples.sentence: The passage praises the critic's discriminating eye for subtle changes in style.
examples.translation: 文章称赞这位评论家能敏锐辨别风格的细微变化。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: She is selective about what she reads.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: selective 强调会挑选；discriminating 强调挑选背后的鉴别力。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The design is tasteful without being showy.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: tasteful 多评价结果有品位；discriminating 多评价人有判断力。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: A discerning critic saw the flaw immediately.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Her comment was perceptive and precise.

antonyms.example_sentence: Indiscriminate praise is not useful.
antonyms.difference_note: indiscriminate 是不分好坏或对象地一概而论。

confusable_words.example_sentence: Discriminatory rules treat groups unfairly.
confusable_words.difference_note: discriminatory 是负面的社会不公平；discriminating 可表示正面的鉴别力。

source.source_meaning_cn: 不 加 区 分 的； 不 加 甄别 的； 胡乱 的 done at random or without careful judgment:

headline.core_en: done without careful choice or distinction

familiar_bridge.summary: discriminate 有“区分”；indiscriminate 就是不区分对象、后果或质量。

core.main: indiscriminate 常带负面，批评某种行为粗暴、无选择，可能伤及不该受影响的人或事。

examples.sentence: The indiscriminate use of antibiotics can make bacteria more resistant.
examples.translation: 不加区分地使用抗生素会让细菌更具耐药性。

examples.sentence: The passage condemns the policy because its effects are indiscriminate, harming both guilty and innocent groups.
examples.translation: 文章谴责这项政策，因为它的影响不加区分，既伤害有责群体也伤害无辜群体。
examples.note: 阅读中常用于批评政策粗暴。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The choice seemed random rather than reasoned.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: random 强调没有明确规律；indiscriminate 强调不加选择和判断。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Careless use of the tool caused damage.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: careless 更像粗心；indiscriminate 更像没有区分对象。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The plan was haphazard and poorly coordinated.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: haphazard 强调安排混乱；indiscriminate 强调选择无差别。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The filter was unselective and let too much material through.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: unselective 更直接指没有筛选能力。

antonyms.example_sentence: Selective reading saves time.
antonyms.difference_note: selective 强调经过筛选。

antonyms.example_sentence: A discriminating buyer checks quality before choosing.
antonyms.difference_note: discriminating 是能分辨差别，和 indiscriminate 相反。

extension.root_affix: in- 表示否定；discriminate 是区分。indiscriminate = 不区分。

word_family.example_sentence: The chemicals were used indiscriminately.

usage_groups.group_title: 不区分对象地使用、攻击或赞扬

familiar_bridge.summary: explain 是解释；explicable 是“背后有原因，所以解释得通”。

core.main: explicable 常用于看似奇怪的结果、行为或模式，说明它其实有原因可解释。

examples.sentence: The delay is explicable once we know that the road was closed.

examples.sentence: The passage argues that the apparent contradiction is explicable in light of new historical evidence.

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Her anger was understandable.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: understandable 更口语，可指情绪上可以理解；explicable 更强调因果上解释得通。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The error is explainable.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: explainable 更普通；explicable 更书面。

antonyms.example_sentence: The silence was inexplicable.
antonyms.difference_note: inexplicable 是直接反义。

confusable_words.difference_note: comprehensible 是表达能理解；explicable 是事情有原因能解释。

headline.core_en: something that brings help, benefit, or relief

familiar_bridge.summary: bless 是祝福；blessing 可以指祝福，也可以指让人庆幸的好事。

core.main: blessing 在正式阅读中常表示某事带来好处；a blessing in disguise 是“因祸得福”。

examples.sentence: The scholarship was a real blessing for her family.
examples.translation: 这笔奖学金对她家来说真是一件幸事。

examples.sentence: The passage suggests that the failed project became a blessing in disguise because it forced the team to rethink its assumptions.
examples.translation: 文章暗示这个失败项目反而因祸得福，因为它迫使团队重新思考假设。
examples.note: 固定表达 a blessing in disguise 很重要。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The policy has clear benefits.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: benefit 更中性，强调实际好处。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The help felt like a gift.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The discovery was a boon to researchers.

antonyms.example_sentence: The gift became a curse.

antonyms.example_sentence: The responsibility became a burden.
antonyms.difference_note: burden 强调压在身上的负担。

extension.root_affix: bless 是祝福；blessing 从“祝福”延伸为“让人感激的好事”。

word_family.example_sentence: The rain blessed the dry fields.

examples.sentence: The scholarship was a real blessing for her family.

examples.sentence: The failed project became a blessing in disguise because it forced the team to rethink its assumptions.

headline.core_en: something helpful or fortunate; also a religious blessing

source.source_note: From archived Unit 1 OCR extraction; source text used only as a headword/page/order clue.
source.source_meaning_cn: 先驱； 先兆

headline.core_en: something that comes before and signals or leads to something later

familiar_bridge.summary: pre- 有“在前”；precursor 是后来事物之前出现、并和后来发展有关的东西。

core.main: precursor 可以指早期形式、先驱者，也可以指预示后来事件的先兆。关键是它和后来的事物有发展关系。

examples.sentence: Early flying machines were precursors to modern airplanes.
examples.translation: 早期飞行器是现代飞机的先驱。
examples.note: precursor to 表示发展前身。

examples.sentence: The passage treats the small protest as a precursor to a much larger political movement.
examples.translation: 这段文字将这次小规模抗议视为更大政治运动的先兆。
examples.note: 阅读中常用于历史因果。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The device was a forerunner of the computer.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: forerunner 和 precursor 接近，常指后续事物的早期形式。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Dark clouds are a sign of rain.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: sign 更普通，只表示迹象，不一定有发展前身关系。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The event was a harbinger of change.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: harbinger 更强调预示未来，语气更文学或正式。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The theory has several antecedents.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: antecedent 强调在时间或逻辑上先出现。

antonyms.example_sentence: The successor improved the design.
antonyms.difference_note: successor 是后来接替或继承的东西。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The model’s predecessor was simpler.
confusable_words.difference_note: predecessor 多指直接前任或前一版本；precursor 更强调为后来发展铺路或预示。

extension.root_affix: pre- 表示“在前”；cursor 和 course/run 有“走在前面”的感觉。

word_family.example_sentence: The argument is a prelude to reform.
The model’s predecessor was simpler.
The theory has several antecedents.

usage_groups.group_title: 后来事物的前身或先兆
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调时间上在前，并和后来的发展有联系。

examples.sentence: Early flying machines were precursors to modern airplanes.
examples.translation: 早期飞行器是现代飞机的前身。
examples.teaching_note: precursor to 表示发展前身。

examples.sentence: The small protest was a precursor to a much larger political movement.
examples.translation: 这次小规模抗议是更大政治运动的先兆。
examples.teaching_note: 阅读中常用于历史发展或因果关系。

headline.core_en: something that comes before and helps lead to a later development

source.source_note: From archived Unit 1 OCR extraction; source text used only as a headword/page/order clue.
source.source_meaning_cn: 虚弱 的 : 无 力 的； 胸 弱 的 markedly lacking in strength: Business is suffering because of

headline.core_en: weak in strength, force, or effectiveness

familiar_bridge.summary: weak 是弱；feeble 更像声音、身体或论证都软弱无力，撑不住。

core.main: feeble 可以写身体或声音虚弱，也可以批评论证、借口、尝试没有力量。

examples.sentence: The patient answered in a feeble voice.
examples.translation: 病人用虚弱的声音回答。
examples.note: voice 搭配体现身体或声音无力。

examples.sentence: The author dismisses the explanation as feeble because it ignores the strongest evidence.
examples.translation: 作者认为这个解释很无力，因为它忽视了最有力的证据。
examples.note: 阅读中常用于评价论证弱。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The signal is weak.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: weak 最普通；feeble 更强调软弱无力、缺乏支撑。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: There was a faint sound.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: faint 多指声音、光线或感觉微弱。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The response was ineffectual.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: ineffectual 强调没有产生效果。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The excuse was flimsy.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: flimsy 常批评论证、借口或材料不牢靠。

antonyms.example_sentence: The evidence is robust.
antonyms.difference_note: robust 强调强健、有力、经得住考验。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The vase is fragile, but the argument is feeble.
confusable_words.difference_note: fragile 强调容易破碎；feeble 强调力量不足。

extension.root_affix: feeble 本身，重点记住“无力、撑不住”的画面。

word_family.example_sentence: The feebleness of the argument is obvious.

usage_groups.group_title: 身体、声音或论证显得无力
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调力量不足、支撑不住，既可具体也可抽象。

examples.sentence: The patient answered in a feeble voice.
examples.translation: 病人用虚弱的声音回答。
examples.teaching_note: voice 搭配体现身体或声音无力。

examples.sentence: The author dismisses the explanation as feeble because it ignores the strongest evidence.
examples.translation: 作者认为这个解释很无力，因为它忽视了最有力的证据。
examples.teaching_note: 阅读中常用于评价论证弱。

headline.core_en: weak in body, force, sound, or argument

source.source_note: From archived Unit 1 OCR extraction; source text used only as a headword/page/order clue.
source.source_meaning_cn: 已 确定 的；不 会 改变 的 unlikely

headline.core_en: fixed, decided, or no longer likely to change

familiar_bridge.summary: settle 是“解决、安顿”；settled 是事情落定或状态安稳。

core.main: settled 描述争议、观点、生活或制度经过不确定之后变得稳定、确定。

examples.sentence: For many scientists, the basic question is settled.
examples.translation: 对许多科学家来说，这个基本问题已经有定论。
examples.note: settled question 很常见。

examples.sentence: The passage challenges what had long seemed a settled view of the period.
examples.translation: 文章挑战了长期以来似乎已经定型的时代看法。
examples.note: 阅读中常用于“旧观点被重新打开”。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The date is fixed.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: fixed 强调不能轻易改变；settled 强调经过不确定后定下来。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The matter is decided.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: decided 更直接指决定已做出。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The rule is established.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: established 强调被长期接受或正式确立。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The dispute was resolved.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: resolved 多指问题或争端被解决。

antonyms.example_sentence: The issue remains unsettled.
antonyms.difference_note: unsettled 是未解决或不稳定。

confusable_words.example_sentence: They reached a settlement after months of dispute.
confusable_words.difference_note: settled 是形容词状态；settlement 是名词结果或地点。

extension.root_affix: settle 是安顿、解决；settled 是完成后的状态。

word_family.example_sentence: They reached a settlement.
The news unsettled investors.

usage_groups.group_title: 问题、观点或法律已经有定论
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调争议结束、判断已形成，不再悬而未决。

examples.sentence: For many scientists, the basic question is settled.
examples.translation: 对许多科学家来说，这个基本问题已经有定论。
examples.teaching_note: settled question 指问题不再开放争论。

examples.sentence: The passage challenges what had long seemed a settled view of the period.
examples.translation: 文章挑战了长期以来似乎已定型的时代看法。
examples.teaching_note: 阅读中常用于“旧观点被重新打开”。

usage_groups.group_title: 生活或状态安定下来
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调从动荡进入稳定状态。

examples.sentence: After years of moving, she finally felt settled in the new city.
examples.translation: 多年搬迁后，她终于在新城市安定下来。
examples.teaching_note: 这里是生活状态的稳定。

headline.core_en: decided, established, or stable after uncertainty

source.source_note: From archived Unit 1 OCR extraction; source text used only as a headword/page/order clue.
source.source_meaning_cn: 和 解； (解决 争端 的 ) 协议

headline.core_en: an agreement that ends a dispute, or a place where people settle

familiar_bridge.summary: settle 是“解决、安顿”；settlement 可以是争端解决后的协议，也可以是人定居的地方。

core.main: settlement 有两个重要方向：法律或政治上的和解协议；历史或地理上的定居点。

examples.sentence: The two companies reached a settlement before the trial began.
examples.translation: 两家公司在审判开始前达成了和解。
examples.note: reach a settlement 是重点结构。

examples.sentence: The passage describes how a temporary trading post became a permanent settlement.
examples.translation: 文章描述一个临时贸易站如何变成永久定居点。
examples.note: 注意 settlement 的地理/历史义。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: They signed an agreement after long negotiations.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: agreement 更宽泛；settlement 常指解决争端的协议。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The village grew slowly along the river.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: village 是普通村落；settlement 更强调定居形成的地点。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The parties reached an accord.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: accord 更正式，常用于外交或政治。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The colony expanded along the coast.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: colony 强调殖民或群体迁入。

antonyms.example_sentence: The dispute continued for years.
antonyms.difference_note: dispute 是未解决的争执。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The matter is settled, but no settlement was signed.
confusable_words.difference_note: settled 是状态；settlement 是协议或定居点。

extension.root_affix: settle 的两条线：解决争端；安顿居住。settlement 同时继承这两条线。

word_family.example_sentence: Settlers built homes near the river.
The families settled near the coast.
Resettlement can disrupt local communities.

usage_groups.group_title: 争端结束后的协议或和解
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调双方达成安排，让争端暂时或正式结束。

examples.sentence: The two companies reached a settlement before the trial began.
examples.translation: 两家公司在审判开始前达成了和解。
examples.teaching_note: reach a settlement 是重点结构。

examples.sentence: The passage questions whether the peace settlement solved the conflict or merely postponed it.
examples.translation: 文章质疑这项和平协议究竟解决了冲突，还是只是推迟了冲突。
examples.teaching_note: 政治/历史阅读中常见。

usage_groups.group_title: 人们安顿下来形成的聚居地
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调人迁入、居住并形成村落或社区。

examples.sentence: The temporary trading post became a permanent settlement.
examples.translation: 这个临时贸易站变成了永久定居点。
examples.teaching_note: 这是地理/历史义。

headline.core_en: an agreement that ends a dispute; also a place where people settle

source.source_note: From archived Unit 1 OCR extraction; source text used only as a headword/page/order clue.
source.source_meaning_cn: 使 不 安

headline.core_en: to make someone worried or make a situation unstable

familiar_bridge.summary: settle 是安定；unsettle 是把这种安定打乱。

core.main: unsettle 可以让人心里不安，也可以让市场、秩序、旧观念变得不稳定。

examples.sentence: The sudden announcement unsettled investors.
examples.translation: 这项突然公告让投资者不安。
examples.note: investors 是常见对象。

examples.sentence: The passage introduces evidence that unsettles the traditional explanation.
examples.translation: 文章提出的证据动摇了传统解释。
examples.note: 阅读中常用于新证据打破旧观点。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The noise disturbed her concentration.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: disturb 更宽泛，可指打扰或扰乱。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The news upset him.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: upset 更日常，常指情绪受影响。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The question disconcerted the speaker.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: disconcert 强调突然失去从容。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The crisis destabilized the region.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: destabilize 更偏政治、经济或系统稳定性。

antonyms.example_sentence: The data reassured investors.
antonyms.difference_note: reassure 减少不安。

antonyms.example_sentence: The policy stabilized prices.
antonyms.difference_note: stabilize 恢复或维持稳定。

extension.root_affix: un- 表示反向；settle 是安定。unsettle = 使不安定。

word_family.example_sentence: The silence was unsettling.
The issue remains unsettled.

usage_groups.group_title: 让人、市场或观众感到不安
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调原本稳定的情绪或预期被打乱。

examples.sentence: The sudden announcement unsettled investors.
examples.translation: 这项突然公告让投资者不安。
examples.teaching_note: investors 是常见对象。

examples.sentence: The film’s quiet ending unsettled the audience.
examples.translation: 电影安静的结尾让观众感到不安。
examples.teaching_note: 可用于心理和审美反应。

usage_groups.group_title: 动摇旧解释、假设或秩序
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调新证据或事件打破原有稳定框架。

examples.sentence: The passage introduces evidence that unsettles the traditional explanation.
examples.translation: 文章提出的证据动摇了传统解释。
examples.teaching_note: 阅读中常用于新证据打破旧观点。

headline.core_en: to disturb, make unstable, or make someone feel anxious

source.source_meaning_cn: 系统 性 的 有 条 理 的； 成 体系 的 done or acting according

headline.core_en: done according to a plan, method, or system

familiar_bridge.summary: system 是系统；systematic 是按步骤、按方法来，不是随手做。

core.main: systematic 强调做事有计划、有顺序、有方法。它说的是方法，不是问题分布在整个系统里。

examples.sentence: The team made a systematic study of local water quality.
examples.translation: 团队对当地水质进行了系统研究。
examples.note: study 搭配自然。

examples.sentence: The passage praises the author's systematic approach to a complex historical problem.
examples.translation: 文章称赞作者用系统方法处理复杂历史问题。
examples.note: 阅读中常评价研究方法。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The notes are organized by topic.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: organized 强调安排有序。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: She works in a methodical way.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: methodical 强调一步步、很有方法。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The analysis is structured clearly.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: structured 强调有清楚框架。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The process was orderly.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: orderly 强调过程井然有序。

antonyms.example_sentence: The search was haphazard.
antonyms.difference_note: haphazard 是没有计划、东一下西一下。

confusable_words.example_sentence: Systematic describes method; systemic describes the scale of a problem.
confusable_words.difference_note: systematic 说方法有系统；systemic 说问题影响整个系统。

extension.root_affix: system + -atic：和系统、方法有关。

word_family.example_sentence: They collected data systematically.
The team systematized the records.

usage_groups.group_title: 研究、分析或行动有步骤有方法
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 重点是过程有计划、有顺序，而不是零散随意。

examples.sentence: The team made a systematic study of local water quality.
examples.translation: 团队对当地水质进行了系统研究。
examples.teaching_note: study 搭配自然。

examples.sentence: The passage praises the author's systematic approach to a complex historical problem.
examples.translation: 文章称赞作者用系统方法处理复杂历史问题。
examples.teaching_note: 阅读中常评价研究方法。

headline.core_en: done according to a clear method or plan

source.source_note: From archived Unit 1 OCR extraction; source text used only as a headword/page/order clue.
source.source_meaning_cn: 影响 全 局 的； 涉及 整个 系统 的

headline.core_en: affecting an entire system rather than one part

familiar_bridge.summary: systematic 是方法有条理；systemic 是问题在整个系统里，不是某个局部坏了。

core.main: systemic 强调整个系统结构中的问题、风险或偏见，不能只修一个小点。

examples.sentence: The report identifies systemic bias in the hiring process.
examples.translation: 报告指出招聘流程中存在系统性偏见。

examples.sentence: The passage argues that the crisis was systemic, not the result of a single bad decision.
examples.translation: 文章认为这场危机是系统性的，而不是某个错误决定造成的。
examples.note: 和 single/local 形成对比。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The failure was system-wide.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: system-wide 直白说明影响整个系统。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The problem is structural rather than personal.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: structural 强调制度或结构造成。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The bias was institutional.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: institutional 更强调机构或制度层面。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The problem was pervasive.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: pervasive 强调到处存在、渗透很广。

antonyms.example_sentence: The error was local, not systemic.
antonyms.difference_note: local 只影响局部。

confusable_words.example_sentence: A systematic study may reveal systemic bias.

headline.core_en: a tendency to favor one side or judge unfairly

familiar_bridge.summary: bias 像天平偏向一边；放到判断里，就是看问题时不够中立。

core.main: bias 可以是明显偏见，也可以是研究、媒体、记忆中的隐性偏向，会影响证据选择和判断结果。

examples.sentence: The survey was criticized for political bias.
examples.translation: 这项调查因政治偏向而受到批评。

advanced_synonyms.difference_note: predisposition 可以是较中性的倾向，不一定不公正。

antonyms.example_sentence: The report claims neutrality.

confusable_words.difference_note: preference 可以只是喜欢；bias 会扭曲判断或造成不公。

extension.root_affix: bias ，重点记住“判断向一边倾斜”的画面。

usage_groups.group_title: 判断、研究或媒体中的偏向

examples.sentence: The survey was criticized for political bias.

source.source_meaning_cn: 使 显得 不 重要； 贬低 … 的 重要 性

headline.core_en: to make something seem less important

familiar_bridge.summary: emphasize 是强调；de-emphasize 是有意把重点降下来。

core.main: de-emphasize 表示减少某事在论述、政策或课程中的重要性。

examples.sentence: The new curriculum de-emphasizes memorization and gives more time to discussion.
examples.translation: 新课程淡化死记硬背，把更多时间留给讨论。
examples.note: de-emphasize 是有意降低某事的重要性。

examples.sentence: The passage de-emphasizes individual genius and stresses the role of institutions.
examples.translation: 文章淡化个人天才，强调制度的作用。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The report downplays the cost.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: downplay 更常用，常暗示有意说得不重要。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The company tried to minimize the risk.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: minimize 可指实际减少，也可指说得更小。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The article understates the scale of the problem.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: understate 强调说得比实际轻。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Officials played down the disagreement.

usage_groups.group_title: 有意降低某事的重要性

source.source_meaning_cn: 对 … 强 调 不 够； 没有 给 予 … 足 够 重视

headline.core_en: to give too little importance or attention to something

familiar_bridge.summary: under- 表示“不够”；underemphasize 就是强调得不够。

core.main: underemphasize 常用于指出解释、报告或理论忽略了某个重要因素。

examples.sentence: Many summaries underemphasize the cost of the policy.
examples.translation: 许多摘要没有充分强调这项政策的成本。
examples.note: underemphasize 是强调得不够。

examples.sentence: The critic says the theory underemphasizes historical context.
examples.translation: 批评者说这个理论对历史语境重视不够。
examples.note: 常用于指出解释缺少某个重要因素。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Officials tried to downplay the problem.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: downplay 可能是有意淡化；underemphasize 只是强调不够。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The summary minimizes the policy’s risks.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: minimize 更强调把程度说小。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The report understates the danger.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: understate 侧重表达上说得太轻。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The theory neglects local variation.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: neglect 更强，可能完全忽略。

antonyms.example_sentence: The article may overemphasize one cause.
antonyms.difference_note: overemphasize 是强调过头。

antonyms.example_sentence: The author stresses the role of evidence.
antonyms.difference_note: stress 是强调、突出。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The revised policy de-emphasizes test scores.
confusable_words.difference_note: de-emphasize 常是主动降低重要性；underemphasize 是给的重视不够。

extension.root_affix: under- 表示“不够”；emphasize 是强调。

word_family.example_sentence: The emphasis shifted to evidence.
The article overemphasizes one cause.
The course de-emphasizes memorization.

usage_groups.group_title: 对重要因素强调得不够
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 重点不是完全不提，而是给的分量太轻。

examples.sentence: Many summaries underemphasize the cost of the policy.
examples.translation: 许多摘要没有充分强调这项政策的成本。
examples.teaching_note: 指出某个因素被低估。

examples.sentence: The critic says the theory underemphasizes historical context.
examples.translation: 批评者说这个理论对历史语境重视不够。
examples.teaching_note: 常用于指出解释缺少重要因素。
